title: centaurea hyrcanica
intro: Centaurea hyrcanica is a rare, medicinal plant species native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Turkey. It belongs to the Asteraceae family and is commonly known as Hyrcanian knapweed. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids content. Centaurea hyrcanica can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is believed to offer health benefits including relief from pain, fever reduction, and improved digestion.
